# ADR-260001: Domain split and sign-in placement

Status: Accepted
Date: 2026-05-20
Owners: Repository owner

## Context

The Crystord product has two distinct surfaces: a public-facing landing site (marketing, legal,
add-on info, contact) and a private SaaS data management application. These need to live at separate
but related URLs and must not be hosted in the same GitHub Pages repository. The sign-in entry point
must be accessible from the public landing page without requiring the user to navigate away before
they can authenticate.

## Decision

- The public landing site is hosted at `crystord.com` (GitHub Pages, custom domain).
- The SaaS application is hosted at `app.crystord.com` (separate host, separate repo).
- A sign-in entry point (form or button) is present on the public landing page and makes an API
  call to authenticate. On successful authentication the user is redirected to `app.crystord.com`.
- The landing page itself never becomes the application shell; it is purely a static marketing site
  that facilitates sign-in as one of its actions.

## Alternatives Considered

- **Single repo, subpath routing**: Merge both sites under one GitHub Pages repo at paths `/` and
  `/app/`. Rejected because it couples the deployment of marketing content with the SaaS app.
- **Redirect-only sign-in page**: Landing page has no sign-in form; it just links to
  `app.crystord.com/sign-in`. Rejected because the user asked to keep the sign-in experience
  embedded in the landing page context.
- **Third "router" repo**: A merger repo aggregates both sites at build time. Rejected as overly
  complex for a static landing page.

## Consequences

- Positive: Clean separation of concerns; independent deployments; standard SaaS URL pattern.
- Positive: Landing page stays fully static; no backend required for the public site itself.
- Negative: Sign-in API call crosses origins (crystord.com → app.crystord.com), requiring CORS
  to be configured correctly on the SaaS app's auth endpoint.
- Risk: If the SaaS app auth endpoint changes its URL or CORS policy, the landing page sign-in
  form breaks. Mitigate by treating the sign-in URL as a build-time config value.

## Verification

- Landing page renders with a visible sign-in entry.
- Submitting valid credentials from the landing page redirects to `app.crystord.com`.
- The landing page itself serves no authenticated content.
- The sign-in URL is set via the `PUBLIC_APP_SIGN_IN_URL` environment variable, not hardcoded.

## Rollback

Remove the sign-in form from the landing page and replace with a plain link to
`app.crystord.com/sign-in`. No structural changes to either repo are needed.
